subject: [PATCH 3/4] btrfs: handle ENOMEM from btrfs_insert_dir_item() without aborting

reviews.author: Boris Burkov (author)
reviews.summary:
Author responded to Jeff Layton's concern about the patch handling ENOMEM in btrfs_orphan_add() by explaining that when ENOMEM occurs, it will instead call btrfs_orphan_add(), which is likely to also fail with ENOMEM.

Author expressed confusion about reviewer's concern, stating they had provided inline comments near the relevant code and apologizing for any misunderstanding.

Author acknowledges reviewer's concern that handling ENOMEM in btrfs_insert_dir_item() might not be sufficient, as it could still lead to an abort in btrfs_orphan_add(). Author promises to resend evidence to support the complexity of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
And when we get ENOMEM, we call btrfs_orphan_add() isntead of
unilaterally aborting. I contend that this call is now likely to ENOMEM

---

I am still a bit confused. I made comments inline closer to the the code.
Sorry if I am being dense or missing the point!

---

Take a peek at https://lore.kernel.org/linux-btrfs/cover.1782249000.git.boris@bur.io/

which I need to RESEND :)

Thanks,
Boris

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch addresses a concern about handling ENOMEM errors in btrfs_insert_dir_item() without causing an abort, specifically focusing on whether this approach will lead to further ENOMEM errors in other functions like btrfs_orphan_add(). The patch aims to provide more concrete evidence that this handling strategy is effective and worth the added complexity.

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary: Reviewer Qu Wenruo suggested using on-stack memory for btrfs_alloc_path() to avoid ENOMEM issues, citing the practice used in btrfs-progs and the small size of the structure (112 bytes), but did not recommend making this change universally for all btrfs_path instances.
reviews.raw_body:
For btrfs_alloc_path(), you can just use on-stack memory for that.

That's the practice we utilize for btrfs-progs, and it's still 
acceptable for kernel, since that structure is only 112 bytes.

We should not switch all btrfs_path to on-stack ones, but for critical 
ones that memory allocation can lead to trans abort, I'd say it's 
definitely worth the extra on-stack memory usage.

Thanks,
Qu

2026-07-18.patch_summary: This patch addresses a concern about handling ENOMEM errors in btrfs_insert_dir_item() without causing an abort, specifically focusing on whether this approach will lead to further ENOMEM errors in other functions like btrfs_orphan_add(). The patch aims to provide more concrete evidence that this handling strategy is effective and worth the added complexity.
